name: Neighbor POP
description: The Neighbor POP uses the P attribute to find, for each point of the input, the closest points to it. It puts the indexes of the closest points in an array attribute Nebr. You specify the max number of neighbors, and you choose the Max Neighbors Distance to look for neighbors. The size of the attribute Nebr is the Max Neighbors parameter. If fewer than that number is found, it puts a special number 4294967295 as the value for the neighbor index. The attribute NumNebrs holds the number of neighbors found for each point. You can output the average distance to the found neighbors, optionally including the point's position. Alternately you can output the Inverse Distance Weighted Average, where the closest points have the highest influence on the average. Instead of outputting the indexes of the neighbors, you can output any set of attributes of the neighbors (careful, this can produce a lot of data). An alternate form of the Neighbor POP takes a second input, where the each point of the first input is compared to points of the second input. Neighbors are determined by distance between points, but via the Neighbors Type menu, they can also be determined by connectivity along any primitive connecting edges to neighboring points (only one step currently). For example, two points adjacent to each other in a line strip or quad are neighbors of each other. ( Distribution - Default, Unique, Closest ) Internally it is using a unbounded spatial hashing algorithm nad put into one of Num Hash Buckets.

parameters.name: Neighbors Type
parameters.label: Neighbors Type
parameters.description: When checking for neighbors, choose to use edge connectivity or only distance to neighbor points.

parameters.name: Max Neighbors Distance
parameters.label: Max Neighbors Distance
parameters.description: Specifies the distance below which a point is considered a neighbor of the current point.

parameters.name: Distribution
parameters.label: Distribution
parameters.description: Determines what kind of neighbor sets are returned.

parameters.name: Num Hash Buckets
parameters.label: Num Hash Buckets
parameters.description: The number of buckets the points will be sorted in based on their position. A good heuristic is to choose it to be close to the number of points.

parameters.name: Output Hash Attrib
parameters.label: Output Hash Attrib
parameters.description: Whether to output an attribute with the Spatial Hash values, which can be useful to debug and optimize performance.

parameters.name: Output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description: Whether to output the individual neighbors and their attributes in array attributes, or their average or distance weighted average.

parameters.name: Max Neighbors
parameters.label: Max Neighbors
parameters.description: Specifies the max number of neighbors. This sets the array size of all the attributes storing neighbors information.

parameters.name: Neighbors
parameters.label: Neighbors
parameters.description: Enable addition of the neighbors attribute.

parameters.name: Num Neighbors
parameters.label: Num Neighbors
parameters.description: Enable the addition of the number of neighbors attribute.

parameters.name: Add Distance Attribute
parameters.label: Add Distance Attribute
parameters.description: Whether to output an attribute containing the distance to the query point.

parameters.name: Max Neighbors for Average
parameters.label: Max Neighbors for Average
parameters.description: Specifies the max number of neighbors considered when averaging neighbor attributes.

parameters.name: Include Query Point
parameters.label: Include Query Point
parameters.description: When on, includes the query point when calculating average when output is average.

parameters.name: Add Prefix
parameters.label: Add Prefix
parameters.description: Whether to add Nebr prefix when outputting neighbor attributes.

parameters.name: Cast Integers to Floats
parameters.label: Cast Integers to Floats
parameters.description: When averaging the neighbor attribute values, whether to cast integer attributes to float.

parameters.name: Neighbor Point Attributes
parameters.label: Neighbor Point Attributes
parameters.description: Creates array attributes the size of max neighbors with the neighbor attributes for each point in the first input.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Neighbor_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
